questions.subject: Stratigraphy & Indian Geology
questions.stem: The principle that, in undeformed strata, older beds lie below younger beds is the law of
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Steno's law of superposition.

**Working.**
Superposition.

**Answer.** Superposition

questions.subject: Stratigraphy & Indian Geology
questions.stem: The Deccan Trap basalts of peninsular India were erupted close to the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deccan Traps erupted ~66–65 Ma, near the K–Pg boundary.

**Working.**
K–Pg (~66 Ma).

**Answer.** K–Pg boundary

questions.subject: Stratigraphy & Indian Geology
questions.stem: The Gondwana Supergroup of India is economically important chiefly for its
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Permo-Carboniferous to Jurassic Gondwana sequences host India's major coalfields.

**Working.**
Coal.

**Answer.** Coal deposits

questions.subject: Stratigraphy & Indian Geology
questions.stem: Which periods belong to the Mesozoic Era?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mesozoic = Triassic + Jurassic + Cretaceous; Permian is Paleozoic.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 3.

**Answer.** Mesozoic periods

questions.subject: Stratigraphy & Indian Geology
questions.stem: The oldest cratonic nucleus exposed in southern India, type area of an Archean greenstone belt, is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The Dharwar Craton hosts classic Archean schist (greenstone) belts.

**Working.**
Dharwar Craton.

**Answer.** Dharwar Craton
